trajectory.thought: Step 1 establishes the foundational premise. HaloTag is a ~33 kDa protein tag derived from a bacterial haloalkane dehalogenase. When CRISPR is used to knock in the HaloTag sequence in-frame with the gene of interest, the expressed fusion protein runs at MW_POI + 33 kDa on SDS-PAGE. An anti-HaloTag antibody will detect any band containing this tag. Critically, in a heterozygous knock-in (one allele tagged, one untagged), the gel produces two bands separated by exactly ~33 kDa — the lower band is the untagged protein, the upper band is the tagged fusion. This 33 kDa offset is the diagnostic signature used to interpret all four lanes. With this established, I can now proceed to the full answer.
